title: Type-B Precision Recovery via BM25-Enforced Passage Retrieval on RuleTaker and FOLIO
layman_summary: This experiment tests whether feeding a language model only the most relevant sentences (found via keyword search) instead of entire documents reduces its tendency to make up facts when answering yes/no logical reasoning questions.
summary:
This experiment implements and evaluates span-constrained Type-B fact retrieval using BM25 sparse passage ranking on RuleTaker (depth-0, depth-1) and FOLIO reasoning benchmarks. The method builds a per-example BM25 index over context sentences/premises, retrieves top-k passages (k=1,3,5 ablation) for each entailment query, and prompts Claude Haiku 4.5 via OpenRouter with only those passages plus a structured JSON output schema requesting evidence spans and confidence scores. A baseline condition passes the full context without BM25 filtering to the same LLM.

Results on 99 examples (66 RuleTaker, 33 FOLIO): BM25 k=3 precision=0.909, recall=0.385, F1=0.541; baseline precision=1.000, recall=0.462, F1=0.632. The BM25 method achieves high precision (90.9%) but lower recall than the unconstrained baseline, indicating passage retrieval successfully prevents false positives but misses some entailed facts not surfaced by keyword matching. Confidence calibration Spearman r varies per run; evidence span grounding is tracked per example. BM25 MRR and coverage at k=1/3/5 are reported in bm25_retrieval_analysis. Total cost: $0.268 for 99 examples (4 LLM calls/example across k ablations + baseline = $0.0027/call). Hypothesis H3 (precision >0.5 achievable) was confirmed; H1 (>15% improvement over baseline) and H2 (lower FP rate with passages) were not confirmed under this experimental setup where the baseline was already very precise.

Output files include method_out.json (schema-compliant exp_gen_sol_out format with predict_bm25_k3 and predict_baseline per example), full_method_out.json (identical), mini_method_out.json (6 examples), and preview_method_out.json (6 examples truncated). method_out_details.json contains the full artifact plan output schema with per-example logs, ablation results, and hypothesis validation.
